goalReverseEngineering.summary: Working backwards from the canonical FIRE number at 4.0% SWR: household needs $2,400,000 of investible assets and $2,191/mo of contribution.

actionability.why: Redirect $7506/mo of surplus to extra principal repayment on the household's $1,219 of total debt. Saves ~$5269 of interest over 12 months at the current 5.85% rate. This action is expected to accelerate FIRE progress.
actionability.doNothing: Without this action the household stays on the canonical projection (2045.6030116358659).

actionability.why: Maintain the current allocation and contribution rhythm. 12-month projection inherits the canonical baseline: net worth $856, monthly surplus $15633, 17.5 months of cash buffer. This action is expected to stabilise FIRE progress.
actionability.doNothing: Without this action the household stays on the canonical projection (2045.6030116358659).

actionability.why: Acquire a ~$1,470 investment property at canonical 80% LVR. Adds ~$41/yr net rent and ~$88 of 12-month growth but draws ~$368 from cash and adds ~$7166/mo debt service. This action is expected to accelerate FIRE progress.
actionability.doNothing: Without this action the household stays on the canonical projection (2045.6030116358659).

actionability.why: Maintain the current allocation and contribution rhythm. 12-month projection inherits the canonical baseline: net worth $856, monthly surplus $15633, 17.5 months of cash buffer. This action is expected to stabilise FIRE progress.
actionability.doNothing: Without this action the household stays on the canonical projection (2045.6030116358659).

gapSolver.summary: Goal Achievement Search exhausted 3168 scenarios. Binding constraint: goal too aggressive.

audit.label: Goal Reverse Engineering
audit.howCalculated: Each required figure is read directly from canonicalFire or goalSolver. The optimiser never re-derives the FIRE number, target passive income, required asset base, or required contribution.

audit.label: Scenario Generator
inputsUsed: scenario dimension lattice (property × investment × cash × property-year × risk-tolerance × target-year)
audit.howCalculated: Cartesian product over a fixed lattice. Stable scenario ids. No financial math — only labelling and tuple enumeration.

audit.label: Scenario Evaluator
audit.howCalculated: Every scenario carries pass-through values from existing engine outputs. No new financial formula is introduced in the evaluator.

audit.label: Constraint Filtering
audit.howCalculated: Each scenario tests each constraint independently and records the first binding constraint. Failure counts surface where the household is hitting limits.

audit.label: Five Recommendations
audit.howCalculated: Each recommendation is a selection over the constraint-filtered scenario pool. No new dollar value is introduced — every metric is the pass-through from the scenario evaluator.

audit.label: Gap Solver / Goal Achievement Search
audit.howCalculated: Walks the constraint-filtered pool, picks the first scenario whose FIRE year is ≤ targetFireYear (or P(success) ≥ 0.5 when no target year supplied). When nothing satisfies, names the binding constraint and quantifies the shortfall via goalSolver pass-through.

audit.label: Efficient Frontier
audit.howCalculated: Frontier picks the best scenario for each objective and flags every non-dominated scenario in the valid pool. No new financial value is introduced.

audit.label: Actionability Layer
audit.howCalculated: Every scenario carries a four-field actionability record: what (candidate label), when (scenario propertyYear or targetFireYear), why (candidate rationale + direction of deltaFireProgress), do-nothing (canonical projected year).
